_comment: Core rules — mechanics that are true regardless of set/patch (interest math, streak gold, HP damage formula fundamentals, bench management). Safe to reuse unmodified when a new set drops. Season-specific rules (comp/level/HP benchmarks that shift with balance patches) live in data/sets/set{N}/rules.season.json.

rules.short_summary: Player ended a round 1-3g below a 10g interest bracket, forfeiting 1g interest that round.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You ended {{missed_count}} round(s) just {{gap}}g short of a savings bonus! Holding a tiny bit more gold earns you free gold every round. You missed about {{cumulative_interest_missed}}g total.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{cumulative_interest_missed}}g forfeited across {{missed_count}} rounds from sub-threshold endings. Avoid 1g rolls or XP purchases that drop you below a bracket — that single gold costs you 1 interest.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: {{cumulative_interest_missed}}g interest lost ({{missed_count}} rounds). Audit for impulse 1g rolls or XP buys that crossed a bracket.

rules.human_readable_name: Interest Overcap — Excess Gold Above 50g Without Roll Plan
rules.short_summary: Player held gold above the 50g interest cap for 3+ consecutive rounds without rolling or leveling.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You sat above 50g for {{consecutive_overcap_rounds}} rounds in a row without spending. Once you hit 50g you stop earning extra gold!
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{consecutive_overcap_rounds}} idle rounds above 50g. Plan a spend — level up or roll down — when you are above cap and HP is stable.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: {{consecutive_overcap_rounds}} rounds overcap without deploy. Match your gold stockpile to your stage-based level plan.

rules.human_readable_name: Pre-Stage-3 Excessive Rolls — Unplanned Early Spending
rules.short_summary: Player spent more than 10g on shop rolls before stage 3-1 without a deliberate reason.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You rolled {{total_gold_rolled_before_stage3}}g worth of shops before Stage 3! In most games, save your gold until Stage 4.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{total_gold_rolled_before_stage3}}g burned on pre-Stage-3 rolls. Unless playing a reroll comp, this gold was better held for interest and a Stage 4 rolldown.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: {{total_gold_rolled_before_stage3}}g early roll spend (pre-3-1). Unless maintaining streak or executing an intentional reroll line, this damages your econ floor.

rules.human_readable_name: Under-Rolling at Stabilization Window — HP Crisis Unaddressed
rules.short_summary: Player was below 40 HP entering stage 4 but spent fewer than 20g rolling to stabilize.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You had only {{hp_at_4_1}} HP entering Stage 4 and barely rolled at all! Roll to upgrade your units and stop taking damage.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{hp_at_4_1}} HP at 4-1 but only {{roll_spend}}g spent on stabilization. With {{gold_available_4_1}}g available, rolling down was the correct play.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: {{hp_at_4_1}} HP, {{roll_spend}}g stabilization spend at 4-1/4-2. Carry 2-stars at 4-2: {{carry_2star_count}}. HP bleed in stage 4 was preventable.

rules.human_readable_name: Post-Stabilization Over-Roll — Burning Cap Gold After Board Is Complete
rules.short_summary: Player continued rolling heavily after their board was stabilized, consuming gold needed for level 9.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: After your main units were upgraded, you kept rolling a lot ({{post_roll_waste}}g more). Save that gold to level up instead!
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{post_roll_waste}}g spent rolling after board stabilization without completing a 3-star. That gold could fund a level 9 push.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Post-stabilization roll waste: {{post_roll_waste}}g across {{over_roll_rounds}} rounds, no 3-stars gained. Opportunity cost: partial level 9 funding.

rules.human_readable_name: Win Streak Break — Avoidable Loss That Reset a Streak >=3
rules.short_summary: Player lost a combat round while on a win streak of 3+, forfeiting streak income.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You were on a {{streak_length}}-game win streak at round {{round}} and lost! Each win in a row earns bonus gold.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Win streak of {{streak_length}} broken at {{round}}. You were earning +{{streak_bonus}}g/round. Losing drops your income for future rounds.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Streak {{streak_length}} broken at {{round}}. Check item placement, frontline positioning, or trait activation at that round.

rules.human_readable_name: Loss Streak Not Committed — Partial Loss Streak Missing Bonus Gold
rules.short_summary: Player was on a loss streak of 2-4 but spent gold to improve their board without converting to a win.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You spent {{spend}}g trying to stop your losing streak but still lost! Save that gold and collect bonus gold from the streak instead.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: You spent {{spend}}g to break your loss streak at {{round}} but still lost. Commit or convert — half-measures waste both econ and streak value.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Failed streak break at {{round}}: {{spend}}g spent, result still loss. Estimated forfeited streak gold vs. spend.

rules.human_readable_name: Streak Threshold Miss — Never Reached +2g/+3g Streak Level
rules.short_summary: Player's streaks peaked at 2-4 all game, never reaching the +2g (5 streak) bonus tier.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You never built a streak of 5 in this game! A 5-game streak (win or loss) earns you +2 gold per round.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Max streak this game: {{max_streak}} ({{streak_type}}). Never reached the +2g threshold (5 streak). Without streak income you're leaving gold on the table.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: No 5+ streak achieved (best: {{max_streak}}). Missed +2g tier entirely. Assess board decisions at streak break points.

rules.human_readable_name: Delayed Item Slam — Holding Components More Than 2 Stages
rules.short_summary: Player held 2+ component items on bench for 2+ full stages without combining them.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You had components sitting on your bench for {{stages_held}} stages without combining them! Combine them as soon as you can.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{component_count}} component(s) held for {{stages_held}} stages. Slam items on any reasonable holder if you don't have a target yet.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Item hold detected: {{component_names}} uncombined for {{stages_held}} stages. Power delta from holding vs. slamming on flex holder.

rules.short_summary: Player's primary carry unit entered stage 4 with fewer than 2 completed items.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: Your main carry only had {{count}} item(s) at Stage 4! Carries need at least 2 items to win fights. Combine your components ASAP.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Carry had only {{count}} completed item(s) at Stage 4 entry. Fully itemized carries deal dramatically more damage. Check: did you hold components too long?
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Carry item deficit at 4-1: {{count}} items on carry. Two God rounds have occurred by this point — audit component choices.

rules.human_readable_name: Item Spread Too Thin — Items on 4+ Units at Stage 4
rules.short_summary: Player spread completed items across 4 or more units, diluting carry power.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: Your items were spread across {{unit_count}} different units with none having 2+. Put all your best items on 1-2 main carry units.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Items spread across {{unit_count}} units (max on any single unit: {{max_items}}). Multiplicative item interactions are lost when items are spread thin.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Item spread: {{unit_count}} units with items, max concentration {{max_items}}. Unless multi-carry archetype, consolidate to 1 primary (3 items) + 1 secondary (2 items).

rules.human_readable_name: Unfilled Board Slots — Playing Below Max Units at Stage 3+
rules.short_summary: Player deployed fewer units than their level allows at stage 3 or later.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You had {{empty}} empty board spot(s) at Stage {{stage}}! Put bench units on the board — even a 1-cost is better than nothing.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{empty}} board slot(s) unused at level {{level}} in Stage {{stage}}. Every deployed unit adds combat power and trait activations.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Empty board slots: {{empty}} (level {{level}}, stage {{stage}}). If persistent >2 rounds, this is a unit-acquisition failure. Were units on bench during these fights?

rules.human_readable_name: Bench Clutter — Excess Sell-Value Locked on Bench
rules.short_summary: Player held 5+ off-comp units on bench for 3+ consecutive rounds.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You had {{count}} units on your bench that weren't part of your team! That's about {{gold}}g locked up. Sell them!
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Bench clutter: {{count}} off-comp units ({{gold}}g locked) for {{rounds}} rounds. That gold could have funded XP purchases or extra rolls.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Bench clutter: {{count}} off-comp units, {{gold}}g locked for {{rounds}} rounds. Opportunity cost at interest rate: {{interest_delta}}g.

rules.human_readable_name: Repeated Opponent Carry Position — No Counter-Positioning Adjustment
rules.short_summary: The same opponent's most-itemized unit stayed in the same hex across 2+ fights without the player adjusting their frontline or carry placement.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: The same opponent kept their strong unit in the same spot for {{fights}} fights and you didn't adjust! Move a frontline unit to block that spot next time.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Opponent's {{carry}} stayed in {{hex}} for {{fights}} fights with no counter-positioning. Blocking that hex or repositioning your own carry is free win-rate.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Static opponent carry position: {{carry}} in {{hex}} for {{fights}} fights. Lobby scouting between rounds should catch this pattern immediately.
